Tests that `Math.max()` returns `NaN`, which is not finite.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the behavior of the `Math.max()` function when called without arguments. The `assert` function verifies that the result of `Math.max()` is not a finite number. Since `Math.max()` returns `-Infinity` when no arguments are provided, the test confirms that the function behaves as expected in this scenario. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific edge case of the `Math.max()` function.
